content.text:
Here is what failed in session [`a191560d-0af5-448c-97d4-09a12f9f4df4`](https://os.agno.com/chat?type=team&id=project-team&session=a191560d-0af5-448c-97d4-09a12f9f4df4).

## Containers

Both are up and serving traffic:

| Container | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| `agentos-api` | Up ~18 min | Port 9900, healthy |
| `agentos-db` | Up ~10 hours | Postgres on 127.0.0.1:5432 |

This was not a container crash — it was a workflow failure plus a streaming bug on resume.

---

## What the session was doing

**User:** `arleif.braganza@devfactory.com`  
**Team:** `project-team`  
**Prompt:** *"design this https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebase-placeholder/issues/26"*

Flow:

1. **Leader** delegated to **design-steward** (issue #26 “Auth”, prior design on PR #27, human comment “Redesign required”).
2. **design-steward** called **`run_design`** once.
3. Router chose **`domain-issue`** workflow → branch `domain-design/issue-26`, **PR #28** opened (6 files changed).
4. Workflow **failed** at the **`post-review-comments`** step.
5. design-steward reported failure and **did not** post the human-review marker on the issue.

---

## Root cause: GitHub review API 422

The `domain-issue` flow died in `post-review-comments` when posting automated PR review comments.

From `flow.log` in the API container:

```
step 'post-review-comments' failed after 1 attempt(s).
...
For 'properties/body', nil is not a string. (HTTP 422)
```

**Why:** `design-reviewer` wrote `review-draft.json` with rich comment objects (`path`, `why`, `fix`, `evidence`, etc.) but **no `body` field**. The shell step maps `.body` directly:

```379:381:workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml
      COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c '
        [.comments[] | {path: .path, line: (.line // 1), side: "RIGHT", body: .body}]
      ' artifacts/review-draft.json)"
```

All six comments ended up with `body: null` → GitHub rejected the review create request.

Run artifacts: `/home/ubuntu/workdir/runs/domain-issue/20260609T103635Z/`  
PR: **#28** on `trilogy-group/rebase-placeholder`

---

## Secondary issue: session stuck + UI resume crash

**DB** (`agno_sessions`): session exists, team run `a8c9368d-ea7b-4227-803c-742790b47198` is still **`RUNNING`**.

**API logs** on `POST /teams/project-team/runs/.../resume`:

```
AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'value'
  File ".../agno/os/routers/teams/router.py", line 236
    "status": run_output.status.value if run_output.status else "unknown",
```

Agno’s team resume streamer assumes `status` is an enum; here it’s already a string, so resume streaming blows up. That likely explains a broken or hung UI for this session even though the agent already produced a final answer in logs.

There were also brief **404s** on session fetch mid-run (session not persisted yet); those cleared once the run finished writing.

---

## Outcome summary

| Item | Result |
|------|--------|
| Design PR | **Created** — [PR #28](https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebase-placeholder/pull/28) on `domain-design/issue-26` |
| Workflow | **Failed** — `run_design` returned `ok: false`, exit code 1 |
| Human review marker | **Not posted** (blocked by workflow failure) |
| Session state | **Stuck RUNNING** in DB |
| UI resume | **Crashes** on Agno streaming bug |

---

## Fix directions

1. **Workflow schema alignment** — Either require `body` in the design-reviewer prompt/schema for `domain-issue`, or change `post-review-comments` to build `body` from `why`/`fix`/`evidence` (and skip comments with `severity: "None"`), similar to `architecture-design/workflow.yaml`.
2. **Agno resume bug** — Upgrade or patch Agno so `run_output.status` handles both enum and string on resume streams.

I can draft the `post-review-comments` fix for `domain-issue` or dig into the Agno version/patch for the resume crash if you want to tackle either next.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
for #1 what is one in other workflows? we have review comments in other workflows
</user_query>

Comparing how other workflows define review-draft comments and post them to GitHub.

[REDACTED]

The other design workflows do **two things** that `domain-issue` skipped. That’s the gap.

## 1. Explicit `review-draft.json` schema in the reviewer step

`architecture-design` and `domain-bootstrap` both pin the deliverable to `{line, body}`:

```280:284:workflows/architecture-design/workflow.yaml
      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json`:
      `{"comments": [{"line": <positive-int>, "body": "<markdown>"}, ...]}`
      — one entry per Suspects bullet; `line` is a 1-based line in the design file to anchor
      the resolvable thread; empty array if Suspects is `(none)`.
```

`domain-bootstrap` is the same:

```280:282:workflows/domain-bootstrap/workflow.yaml
      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json`:
      `{"comments": [{"line": <positive-int>, "body": "<markdown>"}, ...]}`
```

`domain-issue` only says “anchored to interface spec paths” — no `body` requirement:

```354:355:workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml
      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json` with review comments anchored to interface spec paths.
```

So the model followed `PROMPT_ARCHITECTURE_DESIGN_REVIEWER` and emitted the rich review shape (`why`, `fix`, `evidence`, …) but never a `body` field.

---

## 2. Defensive `post-review-comments` shell (loop, not one-liner)

The other workflows use a **bash loop** that:

1. Reads `.body` per comment
2. **Skips empty bodies** (`[[ -n "${body}" ]] || continue`)
3. **Dedupes** against existing PR thread bodies
4. Builds the GitHub payload incrementally
5. **No-ops** if nothing new to post

From `architecture-design`:

```311:330:workflows/architecture-design/workflow.yaml
      COMMENTS_JSON='[]'
      for ((i = 0; i < COUNT; i++)); do
        body="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].body" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        line="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].line // 1" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        [[ -n "${body}" ]] || continue
        if printf '%s\n' "${EXISTING_BODIES}" | grep -Fxq "${body}"; then
          continue
        fi
        COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c \
          --arg path "${DESIGN_PATH}" \
          --argjson line "${line}" \
          --arg body "${body}" \
          '. + [{path: $path, line: $line, side: "RIGHT", body: $body}]' \
          <<< "${COMMENTS_JSON}")"
      done

      NEW_COUNT="$(jq 'length' <<< "${COMMENTS_JSON}")"
      if [[ "${NEW_COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi
```

`domain-issue` uses a one-shot jq that blindly maps `.body` — nulls go straight to GitHub → 422:

```379:381:workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml
      COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c '
        [.comments[] | {path: .path, line: (.line // 1), side: "RIGHT", body: .body}]
      ' artifacts/review-draft.json)"
```

---

## Intended pattern (per spec)

`domain-driven/spec.md` says shell steps should be copied from `architecture-design` verbatim in structure. `domain-issue` drifted on both the deliverable prompt and the post step.

---

## What to copy for `domain-issue`

| Piece | `architecture-design` / `domain-bootstrap` | `domain-issue` today |
|-------|---------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| Reviewer deliverable | `{line, body}` JSON schema | vague “anchored to paths” |
| Post step | loop + skip empty + dedupe | one-liner jq |
| Comment path | single `design_path` for all comments | per-comment `.path` (correct for multi-domain) |

So the fix is not inventing a new pattern — it’s aligning `domain-issue` with the existing one, with one intentional difference: use `.comments[$i].path` instead of a fixed `DESIGN_PATH`, since comments land on different `interface.md` files.

`coder` is different: it does internal review (`review1.md` on devpod), not GitHub PR review comments, so it’s not the model here.

Want me to patch `domain-issue/workflow.yaml` to match `architecture-design`/`domain-bootstrap`?

[REDACTED]

Aligning `domain-issue` with the architecture-design review-comment pattern—reading the full workflow sections first.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
## Context
      Routing: `artifacts/routing.json`. Interface specs under `source/design/domains/*/interface.md`.
      Review threads: `artifacts/review-threads.json`.

      PROMPT_ARCHITECTURE_DESIGN_REVIEWER

      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json` with review comments anchored to interface spec paths.
input.new_string:
## Context
      Routing: `artifacts/routing.json`. Interface specs under `source/design/domains/*/interface.md`.
      Existing resolvable review threads: `artifacts/review-threads.json` (do not duplicate
      `root_body` text). Verify claims against `source/` (read-only).

      PROMPT_ARCHITECTURE_DESIGN_REVIEWER

      ## Deliverable
      Write only `artifacts/review-draft.json`:
      `{"comments": [{"path": "source/design/domains/<domain>/interface.md", "line": <positive-int>, "body": "<markdown>"}, ...]}`
      — one entry per Suspects bullet; `path` is the interface spec file; `line` is a 1-based
      line in that file to anchor the resolvable thread; empty array if Suspects is `(none)`.

input.old_string:
OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      COUNT="$(jq '.comments | length' artifacts/review-draft.json)"
      if [[ "${COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      COMMIT_ID="$(gh pr view "${PR_NUM}" --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --json headRefOid -q .headRefOid)"
      COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c '
        [.comments[] | {path: .path, line: (.line // 1), side: "RIGHT", body: .body}]
      ' artifacts/review-draft.json)"

      jq -n \
        --arg commit_id "${COMMIT_ID}" \
        --argjson comments "${COMMENTS_JSON}" \
        '{commit_id: $commit_id, event: "COMMENT", body: "Domain issue design review (automated)", comments: $comments}' \
        | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUM}/reviews" --input -
      printf '%s' "${COUNT}" > artifacts/posted-count.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/posted-count.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: test -f artifacts/posted-count.txt
input.new_string:
OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"

      COUNT="$(jq '.comments | length' artifacts/review-draft.json)"
      if [[ "${COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      COMMIT_ID="$(gh pr view "${PR_NUM}" --repo "${OWNER}/${REPO}" --json headRefOid -q .headRefOid)"
      EXISTING_BODIES="$(jq -r '[.threads[].root_body] | .[]' artifacts/review-threads.json)"

      COMMENTS_JSON='[]'
      for ((i = 0; i < COUNT; i++)); do
        path="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].path" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        body="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].body" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        line="$(jq -r ".comments[$i].line // 1" artifacts/review-draft.json)"
        [[ -n "${path}" && -n "${body}" ]] || continue
        if printf '%s\n' "${EXISTING_BODIES}" | grep -Fxq "${body}"; then
          continue
        fi
        COMMENTS_JSON="$(jq -c \
          --arg path "${path}" \
          --argjson line "${line}" \
          --arg body "${body}" \
          '. + [{path: $path, line: $line, side: "RIGHT", body: $body}]' \
          <<< "${COMMENTS_JSON}")"
      done

      NEW_COUNT="$(jq 'length' <<< "${COMMENTS_JSON}")"
      if [[ "${NEW_COUNT}" -eq 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/posted-count.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      jq -n \
        --arg commit_id "${COMMIT_ID}" \
        --argjson comments "${COMMENTS_JSON}" \
        '{commit_id: $commit_id, event: "COMMENT", body: "Domain issue design review (automated)", comments: $comments}' \
        | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUM}/reviews" --input -
      printf '%s' "${NEW_COUNT}" > artifacts/posted-count.txt
    produces:
      - artifacts/posted-count.txt
    verify:
      policy: shell-command
      command: >-
        test -f artifacts/posted-count.txt &&
        jq -e '.comments | type == "array"' artifacts/review-draft.json

content.text:
Patched `workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml` to match `architecture-design` / `domain-bootstrap`.

### `design-reviewer` step
- Added “do not duplicate `root_body`” context (same as the other workflows).
- Replaced the vague deliverable with an explicit schema:
  ```json
  {"comments": [{"path": "source/design/domains/<domain>/interface.md", "line": <int>, "body": "<markdown>"}, ...]}
  ```
- One entry per Suspects bullet; empty array if none.

### `post-review-comments` step
- Replaced the one-liner `jq` with the loop pattern from `architecture-design`:
  - Skip comments with empty `path` or `body`
  - Dedupe against existing PR thread bodies
  - Use per-comment `.path` (not a single `design_path`)
  - No-op when nothing new to post
  - Write `posted-count.txt` with the actual posted count
- Verify step now also checks `review-draft.json` has a `comments` array.

The only intentional difference from `architecture-design` is per-comment `path` instead of a fixed `DESIGN_PATH`, since domain-issue comments land on multiple `interface.md` files.

Re-run design on issue #26 to validate end-to-end (or rebuild/restart `agentos-api` if the container serves workflow YAML from the image rather than a mount).

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
unlike the older design pipeline, domain design is not putting the link to the design doc in the issue comment. it writes this
==++
Domain design
Affected domains: identity, catalog
Branch: domain-design/issue-26
===========
</user_query>

Comparing how the legacy and domain-driven workflows post design summaries on issues.

[REDACTED]

Updating `post-design-comment` in `domain-issue` to include per-domain interface spec links, matching `architecture-design`.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
- id: post-design-comment
    kind: shell
    name: Post first PR comment with routing summary
    requires: [open-pr]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      BRANCH="$(jq -r .branch artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"
      MARKER="<!-- domain-issue:design-summary -->"

      EXISTING="$(gh api "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" \
        --jq "[.[] | select(.body | contains(\"${MARKER}\"))] | length")"
      if [[ "${EXISTING}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      DOMAINS="$(jq -r '.affected_domains | join(", ")' artifacts/routing.json)"
      jq -n \
        --arg marker "${MARKER}" \
        --arg domains "${DOMAINS}" \
        --arg branch "${BRANCH}" \
        '{
          body: (
            $marker + "\n\n## Domain design\n\n"
            + "Affected domains: " + $domains + "\n"
            + "Branch: `" + $branch + "`\n"
          )
        }' | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" --input -
      printf '1' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
input.new_string:
- id: post-design-comment
    kind: shell
    name: Post first PR comment with links to domain interface specs on branch
    requires: [open-pr]
    prompt: |
      set -euo pipefail
      OWNER="$(jq -r .repo_owner artifacts/acquire.json)"
      REPO="$(jq -r .repo_name artifacts/acquire.json)"
      BRANCH="$(jq -r .branch artifacts/acquire.json)"
      PR_NUM="$(cat artifacts/pr-number.txt)"
      MARKER="<!-- domain-issue:design-summary -->"

      EXISTING="$(gh api "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" \
        --jq "[.[] | select(.body | contains(\"${MARKER}\"))] | length")"
      if [[ "${EXISTING}" -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf '0' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt
        exit 0
      fi

      DOMAINS="$(jq -r '.affected_domains | join(", ")' artifacts/routing.json)"
      SPEC_LINKS="$(jq -r \
        --arg owner "${OWNER}" \
        --arg repo "${REPO}" \
        --arg branch "${BRANCH}" \
        '
          [.affected_domains[]
            | "source/design/domains/\(.)/interface.md" as $path
            | "- [" + $path + "](https://github.com/" + $owner + "/" + $repo + "/blob/" + $branch + "/" + $path + ")"
          ] | join("\n")
        ' artifacts/routing.json)"
      jq -n \
        --arg marker "${MARKER}" \
        --arg domains "${DOMAINS}" \
        --arg branch "${BRANCH}" \
        --arg links "${SPEC_LINKS}" \
        '{
          body: (
            $marker + "\n\n## Domain design\n\n"
            + "Affected domains: " + $domains + "\n\n"
            + "View on branch:\n" + $links + "\n\n"
            + "Branch: `" + $branch + "`\n"
          )
        }' | gh api -X POST "repos/${OWNER}/${REPO}/issues/${PR_NUM}/comments" --input -
      printf '1' > artifacts/design-comment-posted.txt

content.text:
Updated `post-design-comment` in `workflows/domain-issue/workflow.yaml`.

**Before** (what you saw on PR #28):
```markdown
## Domain design

Affected domains: identity, catalog
Branch: `domain-design/issue-26`
```

**After** (matches `architecture-design` / `domain-bootstrap` pattern):
```markdown
## Domain design

Affected domains: identity, catalog

View on branch:
- [source/design/domains/identity/interface.md](https://github.com/.../blob/domain-design/issue-26/source/design/domains/identity/interface.md)
- [source/design/domains/catalog/interface.md](https://github.com/.../blob/domain-design/issue-26/source/design/domains/catalog/interface.md)

Branch: `domain-design/issue-26`
```

Links are built from `artifacts/routing.json` → `affected_domains`, one blob URL per domain `interface.md`. `domain-bootstrap` already linked `design/domain-map.md`; only `domain-issue` was missing this.

**Note:** This comment is posted on the **PR timeline** (same as the legacy pipeline’s design-summary comment). The separate **issue** human-review marker (`<!-- project-team:human-review -->`) is still posted by design-steward after a successful run — that didn’t happen on issue #26 because the workflow failed earlier.

Rebuild/restart `agentos-api` for the change to take effect in the running container.
